title: Chlamydia, gonorrhea and syphilis in Canada - Canada.ca
content:
## Chlamydia, gonorrhea and syphilis in Canada
Download the alternative format (PDF format, 1.06 MB, 1 page)
Organization: Public Health Agency of Canada
Published: 2019-02-11
Rates  of chlamydia have increased by 49% from 2007 to 2016. The following table  presents chlamydia rates per 100,000 population by age group.
Three  quarters (76%) of chlamydia infections are among 15-29 year olds (2016) Footnote 1
The  following table presents the average annual crude rates of reported chlamydia cases  per 100,000 population by province and territory (2012-2016).
Province or Territory
Average crude rate 2012-2016
Rates  of gonorrhea have increased by 81% from 2007 to 2016. The following table  presents gonorrhea rates per 100,000 population by age group.
Table 1. Rates of gonorrhea per  100,000 population by age group (2007-2016) Footnote 1
More  than half (57%) of gonorrhea infections are among 15-29 year olds (2016) Footnote 1 .
Multidrug-resistant  gonorrhoea is an emerging issue.
The  following table presents the average annual crude rates of reported gonorrhea cases  per 100,000 population by province and territory (2012-2016).
Province or Territory
Average crude rate 2012-2016
Rates  of syphilis have increased by 178% from 2007 to 2016. The following table  presents syphilis rates per 100,000 population by age group.
The  vast majority (92%) of syphilis infections are among men (2016) Footnote 1 .
The  following table presents the average annual crude rates of reported syphilis  cases per 100,000 population by province and territory (2012-2016).
Province or Territory
Average crude rate 2012-2016
1.	PHAC. (2018). Canadian Notiﬁable Diseases Surveillance System
